other_observers: None; This bird was originally found by a DFO field trip the day before I observed the bird.
specific_location: Along the South Platte River south of the intersection of Sante Fe and Bowles.
description: The duck was feeding in the grass with a flock of American Widgeon. It had a rufous colored head that was slightly darker towards the rear of the head. The forehead was a buffy yellowish color running longitudinally from the top of the bill upwards to the top of the head. The bill was silvery gray with a black tip. The breast had a rose colored hue. The back was a medium gray leading down to black tertial feathers. The flanks were a lighter gray with a horizontal white line that appeared between the flanks and scapulars. The belly was white. The undertail coverts and uppertail coverts were black. The tail feathers were darker gray coming to a point. The legs were dark.Behaviors: The bird was walking about munching on grass with other ducks.Call: N/APlumage: Adult Breeding
